item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents oppose the statement from the start and remain opposed through the final turn. Agent A repeatedly rejects automatic priority for society while allowing that common interests matter under rights-based limits. Agent B mirrors that rights-first stance, engaging Agent A's minority-protection and abuse-of-public-interest concerns. No visible position flip occurs; concessions are narrow and do not change either agent's stance.
